info.name: Track.IO Backend API
info.description:
Complete API collection for Track.IO Backend Receptor application. This collection includes all endpoints for managing areas, departments, devices, device signals, measurements, raw signals, raw measurements, and the complete alert monitoring system.

## Base URL
Default: `http://localhost:3000`

## Features
- **Areas Management**: CRUD operations for areas
- **Departments Management**: CRUD operations for departments
- **Devices Management**: CRUD operations for devices
- **Device Signals Management**: CRUD operations for device signals
- **Measurements Management**: CRUD operations for measurements (configured measurement definitions)
- **Raw Signals**: Process and retrieve raw signal data
- **Raw Measurements**: Process and retrieve raw measurement data
- **Alert System**: Complete alert monitoring with rules, messages, torretas, receptors and triggers

## Authentication
Currently no authentication is required.

## Response Format
All responses follow this structure:
```json
{
  "message": "Operation description",
  "data": {},
  "total": 0,
  "pagination": {
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0,
    "total": 0
  }
}
```

item.name: Areas
item.description: Endpoints for managing geographical or organizational areas

request.description: Creates a new area in the system.
Retrieves a paginated list of all areas with optional filters.
Returns the total count of areas in the system.
Retrieves a specific area by its ID.
Updates an existing area.
Soft deletes an area (sets deletedAt timestamp).
Restores a soft-deleted area.
Creates a new department in the system.
Retrieves a paginated list of all departments with optional filters.
Returns the total count of departments in the system.
Retrieves a specific department by its ID.
Updates an existing department.
Soft deletes a department (sets deletedAt timestamp).
Restores a soft-deleted department.
Creates a new device in the system.
Retrieves a paginated list of all devices with optional filters.
Returns the total count of devices in the system.
Returns the count of devices in a specific area.
Retrieves all devices in a specific area.
Retrieves a device by its external ID.
Retrieves a specific device by its ID.
Updates an existing device.
Soft deletes a device (sets deletedAt timestamp).
Restores a soft-deleted device.

item.name: Device Signals
item.description: Endpoints for managing device signals (configured signal mappings)

request.description: Creates a new device signal configuration.
Retrieves a paginated list of all device signals with optional filters.
Returns the total count of device signals in the system.

item.name: Get Device Signals Count by Device

request.description: Returns the count of signals for a specific device.

item.name: Get Device Signals Count by Department

request.description: Returns the count of signals for a specific department.
Retrieves all signals for a specific device.
Retrieves all signals for a specific department.

item.name: Get Device Signal by External Value ID

request.description: Retrieves a device signal by its external value ID.
Retrieves a specific device signal by its ID.
Updates an existing device signal.
Soft deletes a device signal (sets deletedAt timestamp).
Restores a soft-deleted device signal.

item.name: Raw Signals
item.description: Endpoints for processing and retrieving raw signal data (time-series data from devices)

request.description: Processes and stores a new raw signal. The signal is saved to the database and emitted via WebSocket with type='signal'.
Retrieves a paginated list of raw signals with optional filters.
Returns the total count of raw signals in the system.
Retrieves a specific raw signal by its ID.
Retrieves all raw signals for a specific external ID.

item.name: Measurements
item.description: Endpoints for managing measurement definitions (configured measurement types with metadata)

request.description: Creates a new measurement definition. Type must be one of: temperature, humidity, pressure, level, flow, vibration.
Retrieves a paginated list of measurement definitions with optional filters.
Returns the total count of measurement definitions in the system.
Retrieves a specific measurement definition by its ID.
Updates an existing measurement definition.
Soft deletes a measurement definition (sets deletedAt timestamp).
Restores a soft-deleted measurement definition.

query.description: Number of latest values to retrieve (default: 10, max: 100)

request.description: Retrieves the latest measurement values for a specific measurement. The limit parameter is optional (default: 10, maximum: 100). Returns an error if limit exceeds 100.

item.name: Raw Measurements
item.description: Endpoints for processing and retrieving raw measurement data (time-series data from devices)

request.description: Processes and stores a new raw measurement. If the id matches a measurement's externalId, it will also save to measurement_values table and evaluate alert rules. The measurement is saved to the database and emitted via WebSocket.
Retrieves a paginated list of raw measurements with optional filters.
Returns the total count of raw measurements in the system.
Retrieves a specific raw measurement by its ID.

item.name: Get Raw Measurements by External ID

request.description: Retrieves all raw measurements for a specific external ID.

item.name: Message Groups
item.description: Endpoints for managing message groups (groups of alert notifications)

request.description: Creates a new message group.
Retrieves all message groups.
Retrieves a specific message group by its ID.
Updates an existing message group.
Soft deletes a message group (sets deletedAt timestamp).

item.name: Alert Rules
item.description: Endpoints for managing alert rules (monitoring conditions for measurements)

body.raw:
{
  "name": "High Temperature Alert",
  "description": "Alert when temperature exceeds 75°C",
  "measurementId": 1,
  "messageGroupId": 1,
  "mode": "setpoint",
  "operator": ">",
  "setpoint": 75.0,
  "isEnabled": true
}

request.description: Creates a new alert rule. Modes: 'setpoint' (with operator and setpoint) or 'window' (with minValue and maxValue).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Temperature Out of Range",
  "description": "Alert when temperature is outside 20-30°C range",
  "measurementId": 1,
  "messageGroupId": 1,
  "mode": "window",
  "minValue": 20.0,
  "maxValue": 30.0,
  "isEnabled": true
}

request.description: Creates a new window-type alert rule that triggers when value is outside the specified range.
Retrieves all alert rules.
Retrieves only enabled alert rules (used for active monitoring).
Retrieves a specific alert rule by its ID.
Retrieves all alert rules for a specific measurement.
Updates an existing alert rule.
Toggles the enabled/disabled state of an alert rule.
Soft deletes an alert rule (sets deletedAt timestamp).

item.name: Alert Messages
item.description: Endpoints for managing alert messages (notifications sent when alerts trigger)

request.description: Creates a new Telegram notification message for an alert rule. Max 5 messages per rule.
Creates a new Torreta (signal tower) message for an alert rule.
Creates a new email notification message for an alert rule.
Creates a new Receptor message for an alert rule.
Retrieves all alert messages.
Retrieves all messages for a specific alert rule.
Retrieves a specific alert message by its ID.
Updates an existing alert message.
Duplicates an existing alert message. Max 5 messages per rule.
Deletes an alert message.

item.name: Torretas
item.description: Endpoints for managing torretas (signal tower devices for visual alerts)

request.description: Creates a new torreta device.
Retrieves all torretas.
Retrieves only active torretas.
Retrieves a specific torreta by its ID.
Updates an existing torreta.
Toggles the active/inactive status of a torreta.
Soft deletes a torreta (sets deletedAt timestamp).

item.name: Torreta Colors
item.description: Endpoints for managing torreta color configurations (predefined colors for signal towers)

request.description: Retrieves all torreta colors ordered by their display order. Includes 8 predefined colors: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, White, Off, Multi-color.
Retrieves a specific torreta color by its ID.
Creates a new torreta color. htmlColor must be a valid hex color (#RRGGBB).
Updates an existing torreta color.
Deletes a torreta color.

item.name: Receptors
item.description: Endpoints for managing receptors (message receiving devices)

request.description: Creates a new receptor device.
Retrieves all receptors.
Retrieves only active receptors.
Retrieves a specific receptor by its ID.
Updates an existing receptor.
Toggles the active/inactive status of a receptor.
Soft deletes a receptor (sets deletedAt timestamp).

item.name: Alert Triggers
item.description: Endpoints for viewing alert trigger history (read-only, created automatically when alerts fire)

request.description: Retrieves all alert trigger records (history of fired alerts).
Retrieves a specific alert trigger record by its ID.
Retrieves all trigger records for a specific alert rule.
Retrieves statistics for an alert rule: total triggers, average measurement value, min/max values, and timestamps for first/last triggers.
